text:
Once upon a time, in a serene forest, there was a wise old owl named Olaf. He lived alone, save for his wise wife Owlelda.

One serene morning, as Olaf gazed out over his kingdom of leaves and birdsong, a faint cry pierced the peaceful stillness. Investigating, he discovered a tiny baby bird stuck halfway through a flower.

Olaf swooped down gently and lifted the little creature from its bed of nectar blooms. Its eyes were closed, and it was trembling.

With careful tenderness, Olaf wrapped it in soft feathers. He took it back to his nest.

Over several days, the baby bird grew stronger. Olaf taught it to singsongs. Their bond deepened; the bird became a friend.

One spring day, just before flowers bloomed, the baby bird took its first flight. Olaf watched it launch gracefully into the air, its wings opening wide.

The baby bird returned again and again. It never spoke but could
